response: {"purpose":"Figure 3d compares the fraction of usable (non-PCR duplicated) reads out of all uniquely mapped reads across three RNA-binding protein mapping methodologies: eCLIP, iCLIP (both ENCODE consortium and published datasets), and other published CLIP approaches (PAR-CLIP and HITS-CLIP). By displaying individual data points overlaid with kernel density smoothed histograms for each method, the panel illustrates how efficiently each protocol converts sequencing reads into unique, informative data. This comparison matters because high PCR duplication rates reduce sequencing efficiency and data quality, and demonstrating that eCLIP yields a favorable proportion of usable reads relative to existing methods provides quantitative evidence for its technical advantages at scale.","title":"PCR Duplicate Rates Compared Across eCLIP, iCLIP, and CLIP Methods"}
